questions.answer: National Income is the total value of all goods and services produced within a country during a year.
key_points: It is also called Gross National Product (GNP)

questions.q: State the difference between Gross and Net Domestic Product.
questions.answer: The difference between Gross Domestic Product (GDP) and Net Domestic Product (NDP) is depreciation.
key_points: Depreciation is the reduction in the value of fixed capital

questions.q: What is meant by the concept of 'Value of Money'?
questions.answer: The value of money is the purchasing power of a unit of currency.
key_points: It is also known as the general price level

questions.q: What is the difference between Consumer Price Index (CPI) and Wholesale Price Index (WPI)?
questions.answer: CPI measures the price changes of goods and services consumed by the common man, whereas WPI measures the price changes of goods and services produced and sold within the country.
key_points: CPI is also known as the cost of living index

questions.q: Explain the concept of Index Number.
questions.answer: An index number is a statistical measure used to express the change in a variable or a group of variables.
key_points: It is a quantitative measure of change

questions.q: What is the concept of 'Chain-Base Index Number'?
questions.answer: Chain-base index number is an index number that is based on a moving average of past years.
key_points: It is also known as the Laspeyres' Index

questions.q: State the difference between 'Deflator' and 'Price Index'.
questions.answer: Deflator is the ratio of the current price to the base year price, whereas Price Index is the ratio of the current price to the previous year price.
key_points: Deflator is a type of price index

questions.q: Explain the concept of 'Gross National Product (GNP)' and 'Net National Product (NNP)'?
questions.answer: GNP is the total value of goods and services produced by a country's citizens within or outside its borders, whereas NNP is the total value of goods and services produced by a country's citizens minus depreciation.
key_points: GNP is also known as Gross National Income (GNI)

questions.q: State the difference between 'Growth Rate' and 'Rate of Change'.
questions.answer: Growth rate is the rate of change in the value of a variable over time, whereas rate of change is the percentage change in the value of a variable over time.
key_points: Growth rate is a more accurate measure of change

questions.q: Explain the concept of 'Depreciation' and its treatment in National Income Accounting.
questions.answer: Depreciation is the reduction in the value of fixed capital over time, and it is treated as a deduction from National Income.

questions.q: State the difference between 'Gross Domestic Product (GDP)' and 'Net Domestic Product (NDP)'?
questions.answer: GDP is the total value of goods and services produced within a country's borders, whereas NDP is the total value of goods and services produced within a country's borders minus depreciation.
key_points: GDP is also known as Gross Home Product (GHP)

questions.q: Explain the concept of 'Price Index Numbers' and their types.
questions.answer: Price Index Numbers are statistical measures used to express the change in prices of goods and services, and they are of two types: Laspeyres' Index and Paasche's Index.
key_points: Laspeyres' Index is a fixed-base index number

questions.q: What are the 'Uses of Index Numbers' in National Income Accounting?
questions.answer: Index numbers are used to measure the rate of inflation, deflation, or price stability, to compare the purchasing power of money over time, and to calculate the real value of National Income.
key_points: Index numbers are also used to calculate the rate of growth of National Income

questions.q: Explain the concept of 'Chain-Base Index Number' and its advantages.
questions.answer: Chain-base index number is an index number that is based on a moving average of past years, and its advantages include the ability to compare data from different years and to avoid the effects of price level changes.
key_points: Chain-base index number is also known as the Laspeyres' Index

questions.q: What are the 'Limitations of Index Numbers' in National Income Accounting?
questions.answer: Index numbers are limited by their inability to measure the quality of goods and services, their inability to measure the changes in the prices of non-traded goods and services, and their sensitivity to changes in the price level.
key_points: Index numbers are also limited by their inability to measure the changes in the prices of durable goods

questions.q: Explain the concept of 'Deflator' and its importance in National Income Accounting.
questions.answer: Deflator is the ratio of the current price to the base year price, and it is important in National Income Accounting as it allows for the calculation of the real value of National Income and the measurement of price level changes.
key_points: Deflator is a type of price index

questions.q: What is the 'Concept of Gross National Disposable Income (GNDD)' and its importance?
questions.answer: GNDD is the total value of goods and services produced by a country's citizens minus depreciation and taxes, and its importance lies in its ability to measure the country's ability to pay its debts and its ability to invest in the future.
key_points: GNDD is also known as Gross National Disposable Income (GNDI)
